recs.s: NEW: 4 copilot-CLI failures today (Smoke Copilot, PR Sous Chef x2, Auto-Triage) all exit 1 after 4 attempts with NO specific error category set. Two of the three failing workflows are gpt-5-mini (Sous Chef + Auto-Triage); the third (Smoke Copilot) is gpt-5.3-codex. Action: (a) inspect the stdout captured in the failing runs (hasOutput=true) to identify the actual error text the copilot CLI emitted; (b) if 'all retries exhausted no category' specifically affects gpt-5-mini today, consider temporarily routing PR Sous Chef + Auto-Triage to a more reliable model; (c) extend copilot-harness error classification to map the new failure signature into a named category.

STUCK FOR 5 DAYS: Avenger hit isMaxTurnsExit=true in 3 of 3 runs today. The workflow's CI-failure-investigation task requires more than 25 turns. One-line frontmatter edit: add `max-turns: 50` (or similar) to .github/workflows/avenger.md and re-compile. Yesterday's recommendation noted ~$1+/run wasted; cumulative cost across 5 days exceeds $5. Each failure today also opened a comment on cascade tracker issue #35532, which is itself becoming noisy. Either raise max-turns or refactor the prompt to be more decisive within 25 turns.

STUCK FOR 9 DAYS: Changeset Generator still pinned to gpt-5.3-codex which auto-routes to gpt-5-codex-alpha-2025-11-07 (404). Smoke Copilot is also on gpt-5.3-codex and failed today in a related way. ONE-LINE FIX: change `model: gpt-5.3-codex` → `model: gpt-5.4` in .github/workflows/changeset-generator.md (and review Smoke Copilot's model choice). Other codex workflows (AI Moderator, Smoke Codex, Daily Cache Strategy) all run on gpt-5.4 successfully. This is the highest-cost-to-fix:value ratio in the queue.

EXTENDED: target=* without explicit number now affects 3+ safe-output tools (add_comment, create_pull_request_review_comment, set_issue_field). Validating at each prompt is intractable. Recommend a single MCP-boundary validator: for any tool whose target config is '*' AND whose workflow context cannot auto-resolve to an issue/PR, reject the call with a clear error message naming the missing field. This converts a silent post-agent failure into immediate agent feedback so the model can self-correct.

3-day rising trend: $6.05 → $9.90 → $11.33 (+87%). Workflow itself succeeds, but the cost slope is unsustainable. Action: diff the workflow markdown / its imported files over the last 3 commits to identify what was added to the prompt. Likely candidates: bigger example sets, accumulated analysis history loaded into context, or a new search step that drags in more data. Cap with either a max-tokens guard or by moving heavy data ingestion to deterministic pre-steps.

Both PR Sous Chef failures + Auto-Triage failure today are gpt-5-mini on copilot CLI. Yesterday all 3 PR Sous Chef runs (also gpt-5-mini) succeeded. Action: (a) check whether the failing PR Sous Chef runs share a PR-input pattern (size, file types, presence of specific markers); (b) compare gpt-5-mini overall success rate today vs prior days; (c) if pattern persists tomorrow, raise with the gpt-5-mini gateway team.

Yesterday's critical did NOT recur today. Today's Smoke Claude failure is a different (target=*) issue. Mark the Playwright/FFmpeg/azureedge.net allowlist issue as resolved unless it returns within 3 days.
17.2% today vs 24.9% yesterday. Baseline restored. Continue tracking; no action.

Day 5 stable for the original ENOENT/anthropic-beta failure modes. Pin 1.0.52 as minimum allowed CLI version in CI when convenient. Note: the new 'all retries exhausted no category' pattern is a separate concern and not a regression of 1.0.52 specifically.
